bibliographic_data.title: Query Auto Completion for Math Formula Search
bibliographic_data.abstract: Query Auto Completion (QAC) is among the most appealing features of a web search engine, helping users formulate queries quickly with less effort. Although there has been much effort in this area for text, to the best of our knowledge, there is few work on mathematical formula auto completion. In this paper, we implement 5 existing QAC methods on mathematical formula and evaluate them on the NTCIR-12 MathIR task dataset. We report the efficiency of retrieved results using Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR) and Mean Average Precision(MAP). Our study indicates that the Finite State Transducer outperforms other QAC models with a MRR score of 0.642.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper evaluates existing query auto-completion methods on mathematical formulae and finds that the Finite State Transducer outperforms other models with a MRR score of 0.642.
core_content.key_methodology: The authors implemented five existing QAC methods on mathematical formulae and evaluated them using the NTCIR-12 MathIR task dataset, focusing on Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR) and Mean Average Precision (MAP).
outcomes: The Finite State Transducer outperforms other QAC models with a MRR score of 0.642.
Evaluation was done using the NTCIR-12 MathIR task dataset.
core_content.full_text_markdown: Query auto completion (QAC) is an essential feature in web search engines, helping users formulate queries quickly and efficiently. Although much research has been conducted on QAC for text, there is limited work on mathematical formulae. This paper evaluates five existing QAC methods on the NTCIR-12 MathIR task dataset to address this gap. The study finds that the Finite State Transducer outperforms other models with a MRR score of 0.642. The authors use Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR) and Mean Average Precision (MAP) as evaluation metrics, providing insights into the efficiency of different QAC methods for mathematical formulae.
